revision.comment: 新しいページ：中塚古墳（群馬県桐生市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川2593 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 25|緯度秒 = 24.30 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 39.22 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺37m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （一説）新川臣 |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「中塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 中塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中塚古墳'''（なかつかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。
== 概要 == 群馬県東部、[[赤城山]]南東麓の丘陵上に築造された大型方墳である。
墳形は方形で、一辺約37メートルを測る<ref name="桐生市"/>。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。截石切組積みによって構築された整美な石室として注目される。古くから開口したと見られるため、石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない<ref name="説明板"/>。
この中塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、地名から[[山ノ上碑]]（[[高崎市]]山名町、国の[[特別史跡]]）に見える「新川臣」に比定する説が知られる<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/>。
古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。
== 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り<ref name="桐生市"/>。 * 石室全長：7.67メートル * 玄室：長さ4.16-4.28メートル、幅1.84メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.74メートル * 羨道：長さ3.10-3.14メートル、幅1.31メートル 石室の石材は[[安山岩]]で、截石切組積みによって構築される。玄室と羨道の間は玄門によって区切られる。
== 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）10月2日指定<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳]（桐生市ホームページ）。</ref><ref name="説明板">史跡説明板。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・桐生市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 10 群馬県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小島純一|year=1989|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[武井廃寺塔跡]] * [[宝塔山古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html 天神古墳・中塚古墳・武井廃寺塔跡]、[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳] - 桐生市ホームページ

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 新里村8号墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川2593 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 25|緯度秒 = 24.30 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 39.22 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺37m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（截石切組積み） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （一説）新川臣 |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「中塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 中塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中塚古墳'''（なかつかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県東部、[[赤城山]]南東麓の丘陵上に築造された大型方墳である。 墳形は方形で、一辺約37メートルを測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められるほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。截石切組積みによって構築された整美な石室として注目される。古くから開口したと見られるため、石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、地名から[[山ノ上碑]]（[[高崎市]]山名町、国の[[特別史跡]]）に見える「新川臣」に比定する説が知られる。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}{{small|左から右に、羨道・玄室。}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：7.67メートル * 玄室：長さ4.16-4.28メートル、幅1.84メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.74メートル * 羨道：長さ3.10-3.14メートル、幅1.31メートル 石室の石材は[[安山岩]]で、截石切組積みによって構築される。玄室と羨道の間は玄門によって区切られる。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）10月2日指定[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳]（桐生市ホームページ）。史跡説明板。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・桐生市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 10 群馬県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小島純一|year=1989|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武井廃寺塔跡]] * [[宝塔山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html 天神古墳・中塚古墳・武井廃寺塔跡]、[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳] - 桐生市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:なかつかこふん きりゆうし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

桐生市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:なかつかこふん きりゆうし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 新里村8号墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川2593 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 25|緯度秒 = 24.30 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 39.22 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺37m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（截石切組積み） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （一説）新川臣一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「中塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 中塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中塚古墳'''（なかつかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県東部、[[赤城山]]南東麓の丘陵上に築造された大型方墳である。 墳形は方形で、一辺約37メートルを測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。截石切組積みによって構築された整美な石室として注目される。古くから開口したとみられ、石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、地名から[[山ノ上碑]]（[[高崎市]]山名町、国の[[特別史跡]]）に見える「新川臣」の一族に比定する説が知られる。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：7.67メートル * 玄室：長さ4.16-4.28メートル、幅1.84メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.74メートル * 羨道：長さ3.10-3.14メートル、幅1.31メートル 石室の石材は[[安山岩]]で、截石切組積みによって構築される。玄室と羨道の間は玄門で区切る。 Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|玄門（玄室方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|開口部 Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）10月2日指定[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳]（桐生市ホームページ）。史跡説明板。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・桐生市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=中塚古墳|title=群馬県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]10|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小島純一|year=1989|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武井廃寺塔跡]] * [[長者塚古墳]] - 北3.8キロメートルに所在する截石切組積み石室墳。 * [[宝塔山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html 天神古墳・中塚古墳・武井廃寺塔跡]、[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳] - 桐生市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:なかつかこふん きりゆうし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese burial mounds | Name = Nakatsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | Location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japan Historical Place Name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei Temple Pagoda Site|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hotozan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]]

The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Japanese burial mounds | Name = Nakatsuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | Location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japan Historical Place Name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei Temple Pagoda Site|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hotozan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - {{Japanese burial mounds | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hotozan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴式石室]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway.
