{{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Burial Mound Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Tumulus | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | Alias = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | Affiliation = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | LatitudeDeg = 36| LatitudeMin = 25| LatitudeSec = 24.30 | LongitudeDeg = 139| LongitudeMin = 14| LongitudeSec = 39.22 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | Dimensions = Side length: 37m | BurialFacility = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperty = | Notes = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square tumulus | Label = Nakatsuka Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == Burial Facility == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway.
