Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | Artifacts = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | Notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | Notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designated a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The dimensions are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == other_designation == === Gunma Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site informational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site informational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_other_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | HistoricSite = Gunma Prefecture other_other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_other_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | other_designation = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == other_designation == === Gunma Prefecture Designated other_designation === * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | other_designation = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == other_designation == === Gunma Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | other_designation = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == other_designation == === Gunma Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | other_designation = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | other_designation = Gunma Prefecture other_designation "Nakatsuka Kofun" | Tangibleother_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus other_designation1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == other_designation == === Gunma Prefecture Designated other_designation === * The site was other_designation1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] -
