{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryū)]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] {{citation needed|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryū)]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] {{citation needed|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryū)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryū)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]]

The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 中塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 新里村8号墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川2593 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 25|緯度秒 = 24.30 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 39.22 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺37m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（截石切組積み） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （一説）新川臣一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「中塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 中塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''中塚古墳'''（なかつかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[桐生市]]新里町新川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県東部、[[赤城山]]南東麓の丘陵上に築造された大型方墳である。 墳形は方形で、一辺約37メートルを測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められるほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。截石切組積みによって構築された整美な石室として注目される。古くから開口したとみられ、石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、地名から[[山ノ上碑]]（[[高崎市]]山名町、国の[[特別史跡]]）に見える「新川臣」の一族に比定する説が知られる。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]54年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：7.67メートル * 玄室：長さ4.16-4.28メートル、幅1.84メートル（奥壁）、高さ1.74メートル * 羨道：長さ3.10-3.14メートル、幅1.31メートル 石室の石材は[[安山岩]]で、截石切組積みによって構築される。玄室と羨道の間は玄門で区切る。 Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|玄門（玄室方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|開口部 Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中塚古墳 - 1979年（昭和54年）10月2日指定[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳]（桐生市ホームページ）。史跡説明板。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・桐生市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=中塚古墳|title=群馬県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]10|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（平凡社）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小島純一|year=1989|chapter=中塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|中塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武井廃寺塔跡]] * [[長者塚古墳]] - 北3.8キロメートルに所在する截石切組積み石室墳。 * [[宝塔山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html 天神古墳・中塚古墳・武井廃寺塔跡]、[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html 中塚古墳] - 桐生市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:なかつかこふん きりゆうし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] to [[Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryū)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] {{translated page|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|version=101165775|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|12=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|12=draft}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft}} - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryu|ja|桐生市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiryu|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|ja|山ノ上碑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanoue Stele|qq=}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from {{ill|andesite|ja|安山岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/andesite|qq=}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Takei temple ruins|ja|武井廃寺塔跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei temple ruins|qq=}} * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|ja|宝塔山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtōzan Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway.
