But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hōtōzan Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|version=101165775|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Gunma]] [[Category:History of Kiryu]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hōtōzan Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Kiryu
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hōtōzan Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|version=101165775|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}}

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hōtōzan Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|version=101165775|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}}

Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Niisato Town, Niikawa 2593 | coordinates = 36| coordinates = 25| coordinates = 24.30 | coordinates = 139| coordinates = 14| coordinates = 39.22 | omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side. The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods. The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979. == burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows: * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).On-site intypeational plaque. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Gunma Prefectural Board of Education & Kiryu City Board of Education, installed 2017) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=Gunma Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japan Historical Place name Series]] 10|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kojima Junichi|year=1989|chapter=Nakatsuka Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Nakatsuka Kofun (Encyclopedia)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Takei temple ruins|Takei temple ruins]] * {{ill|Chojazuka Kofun|ja|長者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chojazuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located 3.8 km north, with a similar stone chamber design. * [[:en:Hōtōzan Kofun|Hōtōzan Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}} * [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/spot/niisato/1001855.html Tenjin Kofun, Nakatsuka Kofun, and Takei Temple Pagoda Site], [https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] - Kiryu City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中塚古墳 (桐生市)|version=101165775|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakazuka Kofun (Kiryū, Gunma)]] [[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kiryū]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)}}

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.

The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters

Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>
