'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in [[:en:Kiryū, Gunma|Kiryu]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]], this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the [[:en:Yamanoue Stele|Yamanoue Stele]].<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

The chamber is constructed from [[:en:Andesite|andesite]], with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|WD=Q634022|lt=Kiryu}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)]][[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)]][[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu)]][[ja:中塚古墳 (桐生市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|WD=Q634022|lt=Kiryu}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|WD=Q634022|lt=Kiryu}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele|1=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022|1=Kiryū, Gunma}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|WD=Q634022|lt=Kiryu|1=Kiryū, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele|1=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-1.jpg | alternate_name = Niisato Village No. 8 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}; {{ill|Kiryū|WD=Q634022|1=Kiryū, Gunma}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.42341666666667|N|139.24422222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-10 | type = {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Side length: 37m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (precisely cut, piled stonework) | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 7th century) | Deceased = (Possibly) Niikawa Omi clan | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Gunma Prefecture Historic Site "Nakatsuka Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Gunma | type = Square tumulus | map_dot_label = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }}
'''Nakatsuka Kofun''' (中塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Kiryū, Gunma|en|Kiryū, Gunma|WD=Q634022|lt=Kiryu|1=Kiryū, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped tumulus Historic Site1 as a prefectural historic site.
== Overview == Situated in eastern Gunma Prefecture on the southeastern slopes of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}, this large square tumulus measures approximately 37 meters on each side.<ref name="桐生市"/> The mound features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, with a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. It is noteworthy for its meticulously cut stone construction. The chamber, having been open for a long time, has no confirmed grave goods.<ref name="説明板"/>
The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the late 7th century. Based on the area's name, it is speculated that the interred may belong to the {{ill|Niikawa Omi|ja|新川臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} family, mentioned in the {{ill|Yamanoue Stele|en|Yamanoue Stele|WD=Q11465812|lt=Yamanoue Stele|1=Yamanoue Stele}}.<ref name="説明板"/><ref name="桐生市"/> The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Gunma Prefecture in 1979.
== burial == [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), 3D-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented to the south-southeast. The area are as follows:<ref name="桐生市"/> * Total chamber length: 7.67 meters * burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 4.16–4.28 meters, width 1.84 meters (back wall), height 1.74 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 3.10–3.14 meters, width 1.31 meters
The chamber is constructed from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=andesite|1=Andesite}}, with precisely cut and assembled stones. A threshold stone (''gen-mon'') separates the burial chamber from the passageway. <gallery> Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the back wall) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), genmon.jpg|Threshold stone (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance Nakatsuka Kofun (Kiryu), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Nakatsuka Kofun - Designated on October 2, 1979.<ref name="桐生市">[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001959.html Nakatsuka Kofun] (Kiryu City Official Website).</ref><ref name="説明板">On-site intypeational plaque.</ref>
